human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for CPG-specific analytics boundaries, metric-definition rigor, traceability and release-boundary handling, supplier escalation behavior, functionality coverage, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to CPG analytics, metric-definition logic, reconciliation notes, caveat design, lot-aware reporting, and disclosure-safe escalation rather than generic BI work.
Authority sources cover FDA preventive controls, 21 CFR Part 117, FDA traceability, recall and labeling guidance, GS1 traceability and logistics standards, and SEC KPI disclosure guidance.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope analysis, metric ambiguity, stale or incomplete data, conflicting systems, unsupported causal and disclosure claims, traceability-aware analysis, quality holds, supplier approval boundaries, terminology normalization, missing facts, and tenant adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/consumer-packaged-goods/cpg-analytics-specialist/ so it remains distinct from adjacent quality, planning, inventory, procurement, supplier-quality, logistics, and engineering lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and readiness/evidence.json as required by the issue.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated CPG analytics specialist for metric-definition, lot-aware reporting, source reconciliation, caveat design, and safe escalation.
Runtime remains specification-based until a retained implementation is promoted separately.
